bestPracticeDescription:
This proactive support capability is provided at no additional cost when you have an active SMARTnet Service, SP Base, Unified Computing Support Service, or Mission Critical Support Service contract for the designated products. Problems can be identified before they affect your business. Spend less time troubleshooting, experience a speedier resolution to network issues, and gain higher network availability.
Cisco Smart Call Home offers:
1. Visibility into your network through diagnostic reports on devices enabled with Smart Call Home.
2. Real-time troubleshooting, alerts, and remediation advice.
3. Automatic generation of Cisco service requests to Cisco technical engineers.
4. Secure and reliable data transport.
5. Personalized web-based portal to review Call Home messages, detailed diagnostics, recommendations, and inventory.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Configure the Call Home feature to receive near real-time notifications of device faults and to help reduce Mean Time to Repair.
bestPracticeTitle: Call Home is not configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
!
bgp consistency-checker {error-message | auto-repair} [interval minutes]
!
error-message :System to generate an error message when an inconsistency is observed.
auto-repair : System generates a syslog and also takes an action based on the inconsistency found.
interval : Range is from 5 to 1440 minutes. Interval defaults to 1440 (One Day)
bestPracticeDescription: A BGP route inconsistency with a peer occurs when an update or withdraw is not sent to a peer, resulting in black-hole routing. BGP Consistency checker will check for inconsistencies in a configurable interval. Once the process identifies such an inconsistency, it will report the inconsistency with a syslog message, and optionally take action if the auto-repair keyword is specified. Three options are available: - Next-Hop Label Consistency Check - RIB-Out Consistency Check - Aggregation Consistency Check Next Hop Label Consistency Check: When two paths have the same next hop because they are advertised by the same provider edge router (PE), they should also have the same next-hop label. If the labels are different, there is an inconsistency. If the auto-repair keyword is specified, the system will send a route-refresh request. RIB-Out Consistency Check: If a network passes an outbound policy and is not sent, or if a network does not pass an outbound policy and is sent, there is an inconsistency. If the auto-repair keyword is specified, the system will send a route-refresh request. Aggregation Consistency Check: If specific routes and the aggregated route become out of sync, an inconsistency can occur. Either the error-message keyword or the auto-repair keyword will trigger aggregation reevaluation.
bestPracticeRecommendation: BGP consistency check notifies and tries to correct routing inconsistency. This helps in preventing blackholing of traffic and thereby improving network health.
bestPracticeTitle: BGP Consistency Check not enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
The following example shows how to enable a Cisco device to automatically delete old crashinfo files if the device needs space for writing new crashinfo files when a system crashes.
!
exception crashinfo maximum files 
!

Notes : The  range is from 1 to 32
bestPracticeDescription: The Enhanced Crashinfo File Collection feature automatically deletes old crashinfo files. This feature, added in Cisco IOS Software Release 12.3(11)T. To enable a Cisco device to automatically delete old crashinfo files to help create space for writing the new crashinfo files when a system crashes, use the exception crashinfo maximum files command in global configuration mode. The number of the most recent crashinfo files across all file systems in the device to be saved when crashinfo files are deleted automatically. The range is from 1 to 32.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to enable this feature in order to reclaim space to create new crashinfo files if the device crashes.
bestPracticeTitle: Enhanced Crashinfo File Collection not configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Configure the router ID, with the "router-id" command.
bestPracticeDescription: The router ID is set to the IP address of a loopback interface (if one is configured). If no loopback interfaces are configured, the router ID is set to the highest IP address configured on a physical interface. To ensure deterministic behavior, the EIGRP router ID should be configured to a chosen address. If a router ID isn't set for EIGRP, it is possible that duplicate router IDs will prevent external EIGRP routes from being installed in the topology table. To have deterministic behavior, an EIGRP router ID should be created on all EIGRP speaking devices.
bestPracticeRecommendation: The EIGRP router ID may be set with the "eigrp router-id" command in later IOS XR versions. Note: EIGRP neighbors will reset automatically when this command is configured. In earlier codes, a high IP address and loopback interface could be used to set the router ID. Configure every router EIGRP process for all address-families, ipv4 global, and each VRF.
bestPracticeTitle: No EIGRP router-id configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: To enable this, add the command "log-adjacency-changes" under the "router isis" section.
bestPracticeDescription: If running IS-IS there is a possibility of getting a syslog message when there is a neighbor status change. This rule is verifying that the generation of such a log message is enabled.
bestPracticeRecommendation: It is recommended for a configuration to log neighbor changes on a router running IS-IS. This will give the network operating center (the team monitoring the network) an alarm if there is a change in the neighbor status on a router running IS-IS.
bestPracticeTitle: IS-IS Neighbor Logging not enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configuration as below 
!
service sequence-numbers
!
bestPracticeDescription: To enable visible sequence numbering of system logging messages, use the service sequence-numbers command in global configuration mode. To disable visible sequence numbering of logging messages, use the no form of this command. Each system status messages logged in the system logging process have a sequence reference number applied. This command makes that number visible by displaying it with the message. The sequence number is displayed as the first part of the system status message. See the description of the logging commands for information on displaying logging messages. Examples In the following example logging message sequence numbers are enabled: Mar 22 15:28:02 PST: %SYS-5-CONFIG_I: Configured from console by console Router# config terminal Enter configuration commands, one per line. End with CNTL/Z. Router(config)# service sequence-numbers Router(config)# end Router# 000066: Mar 22 15:35:57 PST: %SYS-5-CONFIG_I: Configured from console by console
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to enable visible sequence numbering of system logging messages, use the service sequence-numbers command in global configuration mode. Because there is a chance that more than one log message can have the same timestamp, we can display messages with sequence numbers so that we can unambiguously refer to a single message. By default, sequence numbers in log messages are not displayed.
bestPracticeTitle: Service sequence-numbers not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: Router will continue to create states under low memory circumstances and deplete memory resources, which can create further problems.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure
multicast-routing
address-family ipv4
oom-handling.
bestPracticeDescription: The oom-handling command under the multicast configuration sub-mode allows the router not to create states when the memory is running low. The following three states are not created when the memory is running low: 1) Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) route states in response to PIM join and prune messages, and register messages 2) Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) group states 3) External Source-Active (SA) states in Multicast Source Discovery Protocol (MSDP) The show pim topology command will indicate when the router is running low on memory and that new state creation has stopped.
bestPracticeRecommendation:
Enable
multicast-routing
address-family ipv4
oom-handling
when the router is performing multicast routing in the network.
bestPracticeTitle: Multicast memory safe guard is not enabled during low memory situations.

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
no logging disable
logging
bestPracticeDescription: Logging system messages to a syslog server is an effective form of fault management. Reviewing the logs and checking for critical messages are important aspects for troubleshooting and incident investigation.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Set up a syslog server and configure devices to send messages to the syslog server.
bestPracticeTitle: Logging to a syslog server not enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: 'logging server '
bestPracticeDescription: Logging system messages to a syslog server is an effective form of fault management. Reviewing the logs and checking for critical messages are important aspects of troubleshooting and incident investigation.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Set up a syslog server, and configure devices to send messages to the syslog server.
bestPracticeTitle: Logging to a syslog server not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: We are required to download and install ROM monitor (ROMMON) version 12.2(11)YV1 before we can use this feature.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configuration as below :-
!
no service password-recovery
!
bestPracticeDescription: The No Service Password-Recovery feature is a security enhancement that prevents anyone with console access from accessing the router configuration and clearing the password. It also prevents anyone from changing the configuration register values and accessing NVRAM. For this feature to work properly, the configuration register must be set appropriately, and configurations have to be stored in a centralized server for recovery if needed. It is recommended to have the service password recovery disable in location where there is higher risk of physical security.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to deployed especially on devices located in physical locations with higher risk. The No Service Password-Recovery feature is an important physical security enhancement.
bestPracticeTitle: Password recovery is Enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: On systems with a WS-X6708 linecard (and possibly others), this feature may be enabled by default but it does not show in the configuration.  However, where this is the case, it is recommended the customer still explicitly enables the command 'mac-address-table synchronization'.  This is to ensure that other cards which can benefit from this feature will enable this feature should the 6708 linecard be removed.   Explictly enabling this command will also verify the aging-time setting in later code and adjust it accordingly.  Additionally, other intellectual captial that will be developed to assure configuration of minimum aging-time values will be dependent upon explicitly enabling the 'mac-address-table synchronization' command.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Below Configuration for DFC Unicast

mac-address-table synchronize

The following is only needed on systems running SXF and earlier

mac-address-table aging-time 0 routed-mac
bestPracticeDescription: In distributed switching, each Distributed Feature Card (DFC) is responsible for maintaining each own CAM table. This means that each DFC learns the MAC address and ages them, which depends on the CAM aging and traffic matching that particular entry. With distributed switching, it is normal that the supervisor engine does not see any traffic for a particular MAC address for a while, so the entry might expire. There are currently two mechanisms available to keep the CAM tables consistent between the different engines, such as DFC (present in line modules) and Policy Feature Card (PFC) (present in supervisor modules):Flood to Fabric(FF)and MAC Notification(MN).
bestPracticeRecommendation: When DFCs are fitted within a chassis the following recommended configuration commands should be entered to ensure synchronization of DFC MAC information.Additionally, it is recommended that the aging time for all VLANs is at least three times the activity interval. In later code, SXH2 and later, this change will happen automatically if the global aging-timer is less than 480 seconds.
bestPracticeTitle: DFC Unicast Flooding Issue

bestPracticeCaveat:
This option is only available from NX-OS version 5.2.1 for Nexus 7000 platforms and from NX-OS 5.0(2)N2(1) for Nexus 5000 platforms. Automated checks are not currently evaluating the version, so exceptions on devices not meeting the minimum software level can be ignored.

This option has been made default post 6.2; Hence ignore exception post the release.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Enable 'auto-recovery' option using the command 'auto-recovery' under vpc domain configuration mode. For example: NX-OS(config-vpc-domain)#auto-recovery
bestPracticeDescription:
To restore vPC services when its peer fails to come online by using the auto-recovery command. On reload, if the peer link is down and three consecutive peer-keep alive messages are lost, the secondary device assumes the primary STP role and LACP role. The software reinitializes the vPCs, bringing up its local ports. Because there are no peers, the consistency check is bypassed for the local vPC ports.
This feature is available from NX-OS 5.2(1) on Nexus 7000 platforms and from NX-OS 5.0(2)N2(1) for Nexus 5000 platforms.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Currently, when vPC peer-link goes down and if the peer keep alive is working, the secondary switch brings down all its vPCs. If the peer-link does not heal and the primary switch goes down and is unable to forward any traffic, then there is total disconnection for the access switches. Auto-recovery will help to recover from this kind of failure scenario, by enabling connectivity to secondary vPC peer.
bestPracticeTitle: With vPC feature the auto-recovery is not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: If a device only has a single image, then that image will be loaded even without a boot statement.  While it is still good practice to have the statement to be intentional about the image loaded, there no impact in those cases.  However, if another image is later added, then the device would become at risk again.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: boot system flash [ flash-fs: ] [ partition-number: ] [filename]
bestPracticeDescription: The boot system statement tells the device which image to load when the device boots. The boot system statement missing from config can result in an unpredictable image being loaded after reboot. Customers who are going through some maintenance windows and doing operational tasks sometimes forget this command and then face a longer period of downtime due to the absence of this command from the configuration.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Configuration a specific image to boot.
bestPracticeTitle: boot system statement not configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
radius-server host 10.5.5.7 auth-port 1645 acct-port 1646
radius-server host 10.23.24.25 auth-port 1645 acct-port 1646
bestPracticeDescription: To specify a RADIUS server host, use the 'radius-server host' command in global configuration mode. Multiple 'radius-server host' commands can be used to specify multiple hosts. The Cisco IOS XR software searches for hosts in the order in which they are specified. If no host-specific timeout, retransmit, or key values are specified, the global values apply to each host. Defaults: No RADIUS host is specified; use 'global radius-server' command values.
bestPracticeRecommendation: 'radius-server host' should have more than one server configured, to implement a high availability for radius-server infrastructure.
bestPracticeTitle: RADIUS host has only one server configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: banner motd
bestPracticeDescription: Usage of system banners provides acceptable use parameters and can have legal basis that depend on local jurisdiction. The 'banner motd' command is used to define and enablea message-of-the-day (MOTD) banner. Banners are not enabled by default.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Use the 'banner motd' command to configure appropriate notices to state specifically what constitutes authorized use and the actions taken for unauthorized access. Do not advertise site name or network information that may provide information to unauthorized users.
bestPracticeTitle: Banner MOTD not configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: 'snmp-server contact '
bestPracticeDescription: The Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) contact is the SNMP MIB variable "sysContact". This can be queried remotely to determine the contact for this device.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Configure the SNMP contact to be the person or group responsible for this device.
bestPracticeTitle: SNMP contact not defined

bestPracticeCaveat: In very large configurations, even if the aggregate-address summary-only command is configured, more specific routes are advertised and later withdrawn. To avoid this behavior, configure the bgp aggregate-timer to 0 (zero), and the system will immediately check for aggregate routes and suppress specific routes.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Router(config)# router bgp < >
Router(config-router)# bgp aggregate-timer 30
bestPracticeDescription: Configuring BGP aggregate timer to zero would trigger scanner run for aggregate routes all the time and it may cause high increase in CPU usage if BGP has other routes to process as well. High CPU usage results in slow performance and high buffer failures.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Best practice recommendation is not to set aggregate-timer to zero under BGP configuration.
bestPracticeTitle: BGP aggregate timer set to zero is not recommended

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Disable finger service if found enabled using the 'no ip finger' or 'no service finger' command.
bestPracticeDescription: Finger protocol requests (defined in RFC 742) to a network device are the equivalent of issuing a remote 'show users' command. Any network device that has finger services should be protected by a firewall or have the services disabled to protect against Denial of Service (DoS) attacks or attempts at unauthorized use. The command 'service finger' was replaced with 'ip finger' but is still available for backward compatibility. Finger protocol can be disabled on 12.0S and 12.0SY with either the 'no ip finger' or 'no service finger' command.
bestPracticeRecommendation: All minor services should be disabled on your system if you do not have a need for them in your network.
bestPracticeTitle: Finger service enabled 12.0 and earlier

bestPracticeCaveat: Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) LDP needs to be enabled for this rule to be valid and ICs is created for single RP.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configuration as given below :
!
mpls ldp
 nsr
 !
!
bestPracticeDescription: If supported, Non-Stop Routing (NSR) can be enabled and will provide benefit for the customer. A disruption in service may include any of the following events: Route processor (RP) or distributed route processor (DRP) failover Label Distribution Protocol (LDP) process restart In-service system upgrade (ISSU) Minimum disruption restart (MDR)
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to enabling NSR causes events such as these to be invisible to the routing peers and to provide minimal service disruption.
bestPracticeTitle: NSR not enabled for MPLS LDP

bestPracticeCaveat: The rule is not checking for redundant RPs.  If the device only has a single RP, this rule can be ignored for that device.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: ipv4 virtual address { [ vrf vrf-name ] ipv4-address /mask | use-as-src-addr }
bestPracticeDescription: To define an IPv4 virtual address for a network of management Ethernet interfaces, use the IPv4 'virtual interface' command in global configuration mode. Configuring an IPv4 virtual address allows you to access the router from a single virtual address within a management network. An IPv4 virtual address persists across route processor (RP) failover situations.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Configure IPv4 virtual address for Network Management Ethernet to access the active RP based upon one IPv4 address.  It is recommended to configure the virtual address in a /29 subnet since /30 is not enough to hold both RP addresses plus the virtual address.
bestPracticeTitle: IPv4 virtual address not configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configuration as below :-
!
interface Vlan1
 shutdown
!
bestPracticeDescription: A management VLAN is the VLAN that is used to remotely manage, control, and monitor the devices in you network using Telnet, SSH, SNMP, syslog, or Cisco?s FindIT. By default, this is also VLAN 1. A good security practice is to separate management and user data traffic
bestPracticeRecommendation: Do not use VLAN 1 for in-band management traffic, and pick a different, specially dedicated VLAN that keeps management traffic separate from user data and protocol traffic. Do not use VLAN 1 for data traffic. Use VLANs that separate user data from management and protocol traffic.  The management VLAN and user VLAN should be separated to prevent the risk of Denial of Service attacks.
bestPracticeTitle: Vlan 1 is used on management traffic

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Enable 'ip prefix-suppression' under the global ospf routing process or an interface level.
bestPracticeDescription: This document describes the Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) mechanism to exclude IP prefixes of connected networks from link-state advertisements (LSAs). When OSPF is deployed in large networks, limiting the number of IP prefixes that are carried in the OSPF LSAs can speed up OSPF convergence. Secondary IP addresses, loopbacks and passive interfaces in the OSPF routing will be exempted from suppression.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable 'prefix-supression' on a router level or interface level to enable the feature.
bestPracticeTitle: Prefix-suppression not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: SCP may be required for secure copy operations. If so,ensure proper configuration of SSH as well as AAA.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: no ip scp server enable
bestPracticeDescription:
The Secure Copy (SCP) feature provides a secure and authenticated method for copying router
configurations or router image files. SCP relies on Secure Shell (SSH), an application and a protocol that
provide a secure replacement for the Berkeley r-tools.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Verify that the SCP feature is required on this network device. Before a user can utilize the SCP server-side functionality,Secure Shell (SSH),authentication,and authorization must be properly configured so a router can determine whether a user is at the correct privilege level. SCP is preferred over RCP,where supported by IOS.
bestPracticeTitle: SCP Server is Enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: no logging console
bestPracticeDescription: With Cisco Nexus software, it is possible to send log messages to the console. However, doing so can elevate the CPU load of a device and therefore is not recommended.  Instead, you are advised to send logging information to the local log buffer, which can be viewed using the show logging command.
bestPracticeRecommendation: It is recommended that console logging be disabled in all device configurations to avoid a situation where the device might hang while waiting for a response from a terminal or situations where the console is needed for troubleshooting, but is swamped with console messages and becomes unusable. Console messages can, however, be useful during trouble isolation. In these instances, console logging should be enabled using "logging console level" to obtain the desired level of message logging.
bestPracticeTitle: Logging to the console is enabled

Problems can be identified before they affect your business. Spend less time troubleshooting, experience a speedier resolution to network issues, and gain higher network availability. Cisco Smart Call Home offers 1. Visibility into your network through diagnostic reports on devices enabled with Smart Call Home 2. Real-time troubleshooting, alerts, and remediation advice 3. Automatic generation of Cisco service requests to Cisco technical engineers 4. Secure, reliable data transport 5. Personalized web-based portal to review Call Home messages, detailed diagnostics, recommendations, and inventory
bestPracticeRecommendation: Configure the Call Home feature to receive near real-time notifications of device faults and to help reduce Mean Time to Repair.

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: logging source-interface $$type $$instance
bestPracticeDescription:
This rule checks for logging source-interface to be configured. This command sets the default IP source
address for the syslog messages. Hard-coding the logging source address will make it easier to identify
the host that sent the message.  To configure all system logging (syslog) messages sent to syslog
servers to contain the same IP address, regardless of which interface the syslog message uses to exit
the router, use the logging source-interface command in global configuration mode.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Configure the logging source address to the management loopback interface with an unique IP address.
bestPracticeTitle: Syslog source interface not defined

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: tacacs-server key Cisco123 or tacacs-server host 1.1.1.1 key Cisco123
bestPracticeDescription: When TACACS is configured, a key is used to set authentication and encryption. This key must match the key used on the TACACS daemon. In later versions of IOS, the key will be MD5 encrypted if service password-encryption is enabled.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Set the authentication encryption key used for TACACS communications between the access server and the TACACS daemon with the "tacacs-server key" command in global configuration mode. Alternatively, a key may be set on a server by server basis with "tacacs-server host {host-name | host-ip-address} key keystring". Any key configured via "tacacs-server host" command will override the "tacacs-server key" setting in global configuration mode for the specified host only.
bestPracticeTitle: TACACS server is configured but an authentication key is not set

bestPracticeCaveat: If other reliable methods of monitoring the CPU utilization are used (i.e., RMON, external polling), then this command may not be required; however, it may provide additional fidelity. This feature is introduced in releases 12.0(26)S, 12.3(4)T, 12.2(25)S, and later. For releases that don't support this feature, the customer may consider this and other improvements as part of their Cisco IOS software life-cycle management process.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configuration as below :-

snmp-server enable traps
(or more specifically)
snmp-server enable traps cpu threshold
(and define the threshold)
process cpu threshold type (interrupt | process | total) rising (percentage) interval (seconds) falling (percentage) interval (seconds)
bestPracticeDescription: The CPU Thresholding Notification feature allows you to detect and be notified when the CPU load on a device crosses a configured threshold. When the threshold is crossed, the device generates and sends an SNMP trap message. CPU Thresholding Notification can be used for process, interrupt, or both, and two thresholding methods are supported on Cisco IOS software: Rising Threshold and Falling Threshold. This command enables the following notifications:cpmCPURisingThresholdIndicates that CPU usage has risen and remains above the configured CPU threshold settings.cpmCPUFallingThresholdIndicates that CPU usage has fallen and remains below the configured CPU threshold settings.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommended to proactively monitor the CPU utilization using this monitoring and SNMP trap method. Specific threshold settings will depend on platform and features implemented. It is recommended to baseline performance of the devices in the network, and from that baseline, determine appropriate thresholds for the specific network environment. As a rule of thumb, though, any time CPU goes above 90% for more than 10 to 15 minutes, this will have a negative impact on operation of the device.
bestPracticeTitle: CPU Thresholding Notification is not enabled

router eigrp 1
address-family ipv4
log-neighbor-warnings
!
vrf vrf-test
address-family ipv4
log-neighbor-warnings
!

bestPracticeDescription: This enables logging of EIGRP neighbor warning, for clarification upon EIGRP neighbor behavior. To enable the logging of Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) neighbor warning messages, use the log-neighbor-warnings command in the appropriate configuration mode. Default EIGRP neighbor warning messages are not logged.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable EIGRP neighbor warning logging and monitor these events to assist in identifying network problems.
bestPracticeTitle: EIGRP Neighbor Warnings not enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure as below for TACACS:

aaa new-model
aaa accounting system default start-stop group tacacs+

Configure as below for Radius:

aaa new-model
aaa accounting system default start-stop group radius
bestPracticeDescription: AAA system performs accounting for all system-level events not associated with users, such as reloads.The aaa accounting system default start-stop group radius command to send ?start? and ?stop? accounting records after the router reboots. The ?start? record is generated while the router is booted and the stop record is generated while the router is reloaded.The router generates a ?start? record to reach the AAA server. If the AAA server is not reachable, the router retries sending the packet four times. The retry mechanism is based on the exponential backoff algorithm. If there is no response from the AAA server, the request will be dropped.The aaa accounting system guarantee-first command guarantees system accounting as the first record, which is the default condition. In some situations, users may be prevented from starting a session on the console or terminal connection until after the system reloads, which can take more than three minutes. The AAA accounting feature allows the services that users are accessing and the amount of network resources that users are consuming to be tracked. When AAA accounting is enabled, the network access server reports user activity to the TACACS+ or RADIUS security server (depending on which security method is implemented) in the form of accounting records.Each accounting record contains accounting attribute-value (AV) pairs and is stored on the security server. Information About AAA: Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (AAA, pronounced ?triple A?).AAA is a a set of services for controlling access to computer resources, enforcing policies, assessing usage, and providing the information necessary to bill for services.These processes are considered important for effective network management and security. Authentication:Authentication provides a way to identify a user, typically by having the user enter a valid username and valid password before access is granted. Authorization: Authorization is the process of enforcing policies: determining what types of activities, resources, or services a user is permitted to access. After a user is authenticated, that user may be authorized for different types of access or activity. Accounting:Accounting measures the resources a user consumes during access, which may include the amount of system time or the amount of data that a user has sent or received during a session. Accounting is carried out through the logging of session statistics and usage information, which is used for authorization control, billing, trend analysis, resource utilization, and capacity planning activities.
bestPracticeRecommendation: It is recommended to enable AAA accounting for all system-level events.
bestPracticeTitle: AAA system accounting is not enabled for all system-level events.

bestPracticeCaveat: It is available in Cisco IOS XR release 3.8 and later.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configuration as given below :
!
ssh server logging
!
bestPracticeDescription: The default SSH server version is 2 (SSHv2), which falls back to 1 (SSHv1) if the incoming SSH client connection is set to SSHv1. To use this command, you must be in a user group associated with a task group that includes appropriate task IDs. If the user group assignment is preventing you from using a command, contact your AAA administrator for assistance. Only SSHv2 client connections are allowed.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to enable ssh server logging command.
bestPracticeTitle: ssh server logging not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: SSO and dual-route processors are a requirement for non-stop forwarding to work. Devices with one route processor support the NSF protocol extensions for BGP. Therefore, it can peer with NSF-capable routers and facilitate the resynchronization of routing information with such routers.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Enable graceful restart using the 'router bgp' mode. If the neighboring routers support non-stop forwarding, then this command will give you the desired results for non-stop forwarding. If the neighboring routers do not support NSF, then this command will not have any adverse effect on the device.
bestPracticeDescription: BGP graceful-restart is not configured. Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) provides a non-stop forwarding mechanism for availability by providing graceful restart of BGP neighbors to minimize downtime.
bestPracticeRecommendation: It is recommended to have 'bgp graceful-restart' enabled under router bgp [as number].
bestPracticeTitle: BGP graceful-restart is not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: Access points running 12.3(8)JA release will show up as exceptions incorrectly.  This release doesn't seem to support the command aaa authentication enable and should be manually excluded from BP reports until it can be done via automation.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
For TACACS configure as below:

aaa new-model
aaa authentication enable default group tacacs+ line

For RADIUS configure as below:

aaa new-model
aaa authentication enable default group radius line
bestPracticeDescription: As described in RFC 2865 for RADIUS and RFC 1492 for TACACS, these protocols provide strong authentication methods to protect access to network devices. Use the 'aaa authentication enable default' command to create a series of authentication methods that are used to determine whether a user can access the privileged EXEC command level. You can specify up to four authentication methods. The additional methods of authentication are used only if the previous method returns an error, not if it fails. The 'aaa authentication enable' should only have a single entry.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Authentication should be configured using a default list method to secure EXEC to network devices. Secondly, there should be a fallback method in the event AAA services are not available. This fallback method could be the enable password or a line password.
bestPracticeTitle: AAA authentication enable is not configured optimally.

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
mpls ldp
  neighbor  password
bestPracticeDescription: MPLS/LDP neighbor authentication should be part of a comprehensive security plan. The MPLS LDP MD5 Session Authentication feature allows an LDP session to be password-protected without tearing down and reestablishing the LDP session. The same password has to be configured on both LDP neighbors, or else the LDP session will not establish. Existing LDP session will be torn down if MD5 authentication is invoked on one LDP neighbor only, or when configured passwords do not match.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable MPLS LDP neighbor Authentication to ensure routing table integrity.
bestPracticeTitle: MPLS LDP Authentication not enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
snmp-server enable traps
snmp-server host
bestPracticeDescription: Traps need to be sent to a Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) trap server. To send traps to a trap server, you must enable the traps and define a trap server.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable desired SNMP traps,and configure at least two trap receivers,but not more than four.
bestPracticeTitle: SNMP traps not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: To restrict access of incoming or outgoing connections over IPv4 and IPv6, the IPv4 and IPv6 access lists must share the same name.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Define an access list and bind it
to the lines default.  
ipv4 access-list abc
10 permit 1.1.14.192     
20 permit 1.1.0.0 0.0.255.255
30 permit 1.2.0.0 0.0.255.255     
40 permit 10.0.0.0 0.255.255.255
ipv6 access-list xyz  
10 permit 40:2004::1/128     
20 permit 40:2005::0/64     
30 permit 40:2006::1/128     
40 permit 40:2006::2/128  
!
line default  
  access-class ingress list-name
!
line template $TEMPLATE-NAME  
  access-class ingress list-name 
! 
vty-pool default 0 99
bestPracticeDescription: VTY lines can be protected through a variety of means. One way should be to ensure that only authorized IP hosts are able to attempt access vty to network devices. Use the 'access-class' command to restrict incoming or outgoing connections to addresses defined in an access list. Use the 'ipv4 access-list' or 'ipv6 access-list' command to define an access list by name. To restrict access of incoming or outgoing connections over IPv4 and IPv6, the IPv4 and IPv6 access lists must share the same name.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Define an access list of IPv4 and/or IPv6 network devices that are authorized to connect to IP services of the network device.
bestPracticeTitle: Lines not protected with an access list

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Remove the unused BGP process via the "no router bgp " configuration command.
bestPracticeDescription: This rules detects if an unused BGP routing process is in a device and should be removed. It checks if there is a BGP process without BGP neighbors defined. Although the amount of resources consumed may not be significant, it is a good practice to remove the unused BGP process.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Remove the unused BGP process.
bestPracticeTitle: Unused BGP

bestPracticeCaveat: None
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
To specify the source IP address of a selected interface for all outgoing Secure Shell (SSH) connections, use the ssh client source-interface command.
'ssh client source-interface  '
bestPracticeDescription: The source-interface configuration affects connections only to the remote host in the same address family. The system database (Sysdb) verifies that the interface specified in the command has a corresponding IP address (in the same family) configured. Use the ssh client source-interface command to set the IP address of the specified interface for all outgoing SSH connections. If this command is not configured, TCP chooses the source IP address when the socket is connected, based on the outgoing interface used?which in turn is based on the route required to reach the server. This command applies to outbound shell over SSH as well as Secure Shell File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) sessions, which use the ssh client as a transport.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Specify the source IP address of a selected interface for all outgoing SSH connections. The 'ipv4 virtual address use-as-src-addr' enables the virtual address to be used as the default SRC address on sourced packets.
bestPracticeTitle: SSH Client Source-Interface Not Defined

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example: The following shows a view configuration and associated community configuration which prevents IP-FORWARDMIB:: ipCidrRouteTable and IP-MIB:: ipNetToMediaTable from being polled. An SNMP query made with community string "mycom" will not access these two tables.
??A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A? snmp-server view cutdown 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.22 excluded
??A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A? snmp-server view cutdown 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.24.4 excluded
??A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A???A? snmp-server community mycom view cutdown
Cut down views can also be created by excluding everything and including only what is required by OSSs.
bestPracticeDescription: Access to MIB tables or objects can be optimized with the use of appropriate SNMP view configurations. This is useful when an OSS makes frequent retrievals of very large tables and there is no direct control on it. To achieve this, an SNMP view has to be configured by excluding respective MIB sub-trees and attaching this view to the SNMP community name or group name that is being used. This approach can also be applied in cases where certain MIB implementations being polled have to be prevented (e.g., very slow response or impacting other MIB performance).
bestPracticeRecommendation:
SNMP server view table access recommendations:
1. In a sparse table, do specific GETs and avoid using GET-NEXT over unsupported objects.
2. In non-sparse tables, use GETs or GET-BULKS to acquire all related objects in one request instead of using GET-NEXTs to walk a table.
3. Consider if objects are interleaved and use this information to efficiently make GET requests.
4. When possible, use objects that provide information about large tables instead of accessing the whole table.
5. Do not retrieve nearly static data more often than absolutely necessary (e.g., at the start of an OSS session).
6. Use SNMP views to carefully select what information is retrieved.
bestPracticeTitle: snmp-server view Not Implemented

bestPracticeCaveat: Do not use the "service nagle" command if you have XRemote users on X Window system sessions.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: service nagle
bestPracticeDescription: When using a standard TCP implementation to send keystrokes between machines, TCP tends to send one packet for each keystroke typed. On larger networks, many small packets use up bandwidth and contribute to congestion. The algorithm developed by John Nagle (RFC 896) helps alleviate the small-packet problem in TCP.Nagle addresses the send side of TCP. It is not necessary to enable it when the device is not expected to be initiating Telnet or SSH sessions.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to Enable Nagle services if device is being used as a telnet/SSH client.
bestPracticeTitle: Nagle service disabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure as below:

RP/0/RSP0/CPU0:router# conf t
RP/0/RSP0/CPU0:router(config)# tcp selective-ack
RP/0/RSP0/CPU0:router(config)# commit
RP/0/RSP0/CPU0:router(config)# end
bestPracticeDescription: If TCP Selective Acknowledgement (ACK) is enabled, each TCP packet contains information about which segments have been received by the remote TCP. The sender can then resend only those segments that are lost. If selective ACK is disabled, the sender receives no information about missing segments and automatically sends the first packet that is not acknowledged and then waits for the other TCP to respond with what is missing from the data stream. This method is inefficient because in Long Fat Network (LFN), the bandwidth delay is large and valuable bandwidth is wasted. To enable TCP ACK and identify which segments in a TCP packet have been received by the remote TCP, use the 'tcp selective-ack' command in global configuration mode.
bestPracticeRecommendation: It is recommended to configure 'tcp selective-ack' command to increase the performance of TCP applications between Network Elements and TCP/IP Applications.
bestPracticeTitle: TCP Selective Acknowledgement not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat:
'security passwords min-length 8' command is not available on all IOS platforms. If it is not available, the exception can be ignored, since the command cannot be configured.

Alternately 'aaa common-criteria policy ' command can be attempted for achieving this functionality.
The aaa common-criteria policy command is unavailable when the switch runs on IP Services license or Advanced IP Services license. However, when the switch runs on Advanced Enterprise Services license, the command works as expected. This limitation is applicable to release Cisco IOS XE 15.2(1)SY2 of Cisco Catalyst 6500 Series Switches.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure 'security passwords min-length 8' in global configuration mode.
Router(config)#security passwords min-length 8

Alternate way of doing with AAA common-criteria:
Device(config)# aaa new-model

Create the AAA security password policy and enter common criteria configuration policy mode.
Device(config)# aaa common-criteria policy policy1
 
Configure the minimum length, maximum length, number of numeric characters, number of upper case characters, number of lower case characters, number of special characters and number of changed characters between old and new passwords.

Device(config-cc-policy)# min-length 8
Device(config-cc-policy)# max-length 127
Device(config-cc-policy)# numeric-count 1
Device(config-cc-policy)# upper-case 1
Device(config-cc-policy)# lower-case 1
Device(config-cc-policy)# special-case 1
Device(config-cc-policy)# char-changes 8

Apply a specific policy and password to a user profile.
Device(config)# username user1 common-criteria-policy policy1 password password1
bestPracticeDescription: The 'security passwords min-length' command provides enhanced security access to the router by allowing you to specify a minimum password length, eliminating common passwords that are prevalent on most networks, such as "lab" and "cisco". This command affects user passwords, enable passwords and secrets, and line passwords. After this command is enabled, any password that is less than the specified length will fail. This command is available in Cisco IOS release 12.3(1) and later. The default value for 'length' is 6. There is an alternate way of achieving this functionality using AAA Common Criteria Policies. For local users, the user profile and the password information with the key parameters are stored on the Cisco device, and this profile is used for local authentication of users. For remote users, where the user profile information is stored in a remote server, a third-party authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) server may be used for providing AAA services, both for administrative and network access.
bestPracticeRecommendation:
Configure 'passwords min-length 8' as a minimum to help prevent password hacking success.  
While the recommended minimum device password is 8 characters in this rule, the Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIGS) requirements dictates that 15 characters are required for all user passwords. Therefore, all passwords for DoD organizations should be a minimum of 15 characters (using a mix of all four-character sets in the first 8 characters and the last 7 characters). The current annual DoD Cyber Awareness Challenge test requires 15 characters as well.
bestPracticeTitle: Security Password Minimum Length Less Than 8

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Enable the Linkup and Linkdown traps with the 'snmp-server enable traps snmp linkdown linkup' command in the global config mode.
bestPracticeDescription: To enable the RFC 1157 Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) notifications, use the 'snmp-server enable traps snmp' command in global configuration mode. When you use the optional 'linkup' keyword, the linkUp trap signifies that the sending device recognizes one of the communication links represented in the agent's configuration coming up. When you use the optional 'linkdown' keyword, the linkDown trap signifies that the sending device recognizes a failure in one of the communication links represented in the agent's configuration.
bestPracticeRecommendation: SNMP traps are vital for effective network management. Enable the linkup and linkdown traps to ensure that notifications for linkup and linkdown events are sent to Network Management Systems.
bestPracticeTitle: Linkup and Linkdown SNMP Traps Not Enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
l2vpn
 logging
  pseudowire
 !
bestPracticeDescription: Logging for L2VPN has not been enabled on the network element.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable cross-connect logging for the L2VPN services.
bestPracticeTitle: Logging (l2vpn) pseudowire has not been enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: BPDU guard should not be enabled on any inter-switch links
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Enable BPDU guard using the global command 'spanning-tree port type edge bpduguard default'.
bestPracticeDescription: Interface Ports connecting to end devices are normally configured as Spanning Tree Edge Ports. Interfaces configured as spanning tree edge ports immediately transition to the forwarding state, without passing through the blocking or learning states, on linkup. It is recommended to enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) Guard on the Edge Ports, as these ports are expected to connect only to clients/server machines and not to any other switches. Please note that BPDU guard can be also enabled per interface, which will be the same function, but we would recommend to enable this global to minimize the chance to miss this setting.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable BPDU Guard on the Edge Ports, as these ports are expected to connect only to clients/server machines and not to any other switches.
bestPracticeTitle: BPDU Guard Not Enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Enable the coldStart traps with the 'snmp-server enable traps snmp coldstart' command in the global config mode.
bestPracticeDescription: To enable the RFC 1157 Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) notifications, use the 'snmp-server enable traps snmp' command in global configuration mode. The coldStart(0) trap signifies that the sending device is reinitializing itself such that the agent's configuration or the protocol entity implementation may be altered.
bestPracticeRecommendation: SNMP traps are vital for effective network management. Enable the coldStart traps to ensure that notifications for coldStart events are sent to Network Management Systems.
bestPracticeTitle: ColdStart SNMP Traps Not Enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure as below for EnvMon SNMP Traps

snmp-server enable traps snmp authentication linkdown linkup coldstart warmstart
snmp-server community public RO 1
snmp-server community private RW 1
snmp-server host 192.168.1.1 abc
snmp-server enable traps envmon
bestPracticeDescription: SNMP: Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) is an application-layer protocol that provides a message format for communication between SNMP managers and agents. SNMP provides a standardized framework and a common language that is used for monitoring and managing devices in a network. SNMP Traps: SNMP traps are used and the role they play in the management of a data network.SNMP traps enable an agent to notify the management station of significant events by way of an unsolicited SNMP message.In this diagram, the setup on the left shows a network management system that polls information and gets a response. The setup on the right shows an agent that sends an unsolicited or asynchronous trap to the network management system (NMS). SNMP Traps EnvMon: Enables environmental monitor SNMP notifications.Envmon--Sends Cisco enterprise-specific environmental monitor notifications whenever certain environmental thresholds are exceeded. Notification-option arguments (below) can be specified in combination with this keyword.When you specify the envmon keyword, you can enable any one or all of the following environmental notifications in any combination: voltage , shutdown , supply , fan , or temperature . If you do not specify an argument with the envmon keyword, all types of system environmental notifications are enabled on the LSR.
bestPracticeRecommendation: SNMP traps are vital for effective network management. Enable the EnvMon traps to ensure that notifications for Environmental Monitor threshold violations are sent to Network Management Systems.
bestPracticeTitle: EnvMon SNMP Traps Not Enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Disable voice-fastpath using the 'no voice-fastpath enable' global command.
bestPracticeDescription: When supplementary services such as hold or transfer are used, the 'voice-fastpath' command causes the router to stream the audio to the cached IP address and UDP port. The new logical channel information that is generated after a call on hold is resumed or after a transfer is completed is disregarded. In order to get around this problem, traffic must go to the application layer constantly so that redefinition of the logical channel is taken into account and audio is streamed to the new IP address and UDP port pair.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Be sure to disable voice-fastpath in order to support supplementary services.
bestPracticeTitle: Voice Fastpath is not Disabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
router eigrp 1
address-family ipv4
log-neighbor-changes
!
vrf vrf-test
address-family ipv4
log-neighbor-changes
!
!
!
bestPracticeDescription: This enables logging of EIGRP neighbor changes. Unexpected neighbor resets might indicate high error rates or high packet loss in the network and should be investigated. Use the 'log-neighbor-changes' command to log neighbor adjacency changes, monitor the stability of the routing system, and help detect problems. Logging is disabled by default.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable EIGRP neighbor logging and monitor these events to assist in identifying network problems.
bestPracticeTitle: EIGRP Neighbor Logging not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: Ping MPLS is not functional when 'mpls oam' is not enabled throughout the Label Switch Router (LSR) network management domain.

MPLS OAM functionality is disable by default, on IOS XR. It is advised to turn on this service when MPLS feature is enabled on the network element. MPLS OAM should be enabled throughout the Label Switch Router (LSR) network domain. Ping commands are useful trouble shooting aids for network operators.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable 'mpls oam', when the router performs MPLS functionality throughout the entire Label Switch Router (LSR) network management domain.
bestPracticeTitle: Ping MPLS for network operators is not enabled.

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configuration as below :-

!
interface loopback 1
 ipv4 | ipv6 address 172.18.189.38/32
!
bestPracticeDescription: Loopback interfaces provide many benefits including increased stability for routing protocols and uniform source addressing for system messaging. Loopback best practice options, the first Loopback address is used for Network Management and Routing Protocol source addressing. The second Loopback address is used for L3VPN networks user-data PE-to-PE traffic over MPLS Label Switch Path.The third loopback address is used for Anycast RP for IP multicasting.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to configure loopback interface to allows routing protocol sessions to stay up even if the outbound interface is down and useful to verify that the router IP stack is working properly.
bestPracticeTitle: Loopback interface not Configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
aaa group server radius SG1
server  auth-port 1645 acct-port 1646
server  auth-port 1645 acct-port 1646
!
bestPracticeDescription: Use the 'aaa group server radius' command to group existing server hosts, which allows the user to select a subset of the configured server hosts and use them for a particular service. A server group is used in conjunction with a global server-host list. The server group lists the IP addresses or hostnames of the selected server hosts. Server groups can also include multiple host entries for the same server, as long as each entry has a unique identifier. The combination of an IP address and User Datagram Protocol (UDP) port number creates a unique identifier, allowing different ports to be individually defined as RADIUS hosts providing a specific authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) service. In other words, this unique identifier enables RADIUS requests to be sent to different UDP ports on a server at the same IP address. If two different host entries on the same RADIUS server are configured for the same service, (for example, accounting) the second host entry acts as a failover backup to the first host entry. Using this example, if the first host entry fails to provide accounting services, the network access server will try the second host entry on the same device for accounting services. The RADIUS host entries are tried in the order in which they are configured in the server group. All members of a server group must be the same type, that is, RADIUS. The server group cannot be named RADIUS or TACAS. This command enters server group configuration mode. It can be used for the server command to associate a particular RADIUS server with the defined server group.
bestPracticeRecommendation:
AAA group server RADIUS should have more then one RADIUS server configured, for high availability
infrastructure.
bestPracticeTitle: aaa group server radius has only one server configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure:
snmp-server traps [notification-type]
bestPracticeDescription: Network Element is not configured to send Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) trap notifications. Traps need to be sent to an SNMP trap server. In order to send traps to a trap server, enable the traps and define a trap server. SNMP notifications are disabled by default.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Traps should be enabled, as required, and sent to a trap host.
bestPracticeTitle: SNMP traps not enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: snmp-server traps sonet
bestPracticeDescription: Enable Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) trap notifications for SONET traps when the network element is actively using SONET technology.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable snmp-server traps for SONET.
bestPracticeTitle: SONET SNMP Traps Not Enabled

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Apply under router OSPF and/or VRF the 'default-metric' or redistrubution with {'route-policy incl 'set ospf-metric' | metric}.
bestPracticeDescription: When configuring redistribution into OSPF, always define the metric OSPF should use with the metric, set ospf-metric or default-metric keywords. Each protocol uses different metrics so it is important to define the cost parameter OSPF should use. To redistribute routes from one routing domain into Open Shortest Path First (OSPF), use the redistribute command in router configuration mode. Redistributed routing information should always be filtered by the policy, policy-name, keyword, and argument. This filtering ensures only those routes intended by the administrator are redistributed by OSPF. For information about routing policies, see the Routing Policy Commands on Cisco IOS XR Software module of the Cisco IOS XR Routing Command Reference. OSPF Considerations: Whenever the 'redistribute' or 'default-information originate' (OSPF) command is used to redistribute routes into an OSPF routing domain, the router automatically becomes an ASBR. However, an ASBR does not, by default, generate a default route into the OSPF routing domain. When routes are redistributed between OSPF processes, no OSPF metrics are preserved. When routes are redistributed into OSPF and no metric is specified with the metric keyword, OSPF uses 20 as the default metric for routes from all protocols except BGP routes, which get a metric of 1.
bestPracticeRecommendation:
metric metric-value: Default is 20 for routes from all protocols except BGP routes, for which the default is 1.
metric-type: Type 2 external route 

Exception Handling:
No Redistribution Metrics Defined for OSPF

Recommendation:
For example, when configuring connected redistribution into OSPF, add a 'metric' or 'route-policy' in one 
of the following ways: 

router ospf 666
 cost 1
 redistribute connected metric 1
 address-family ipv4 unicast
 vrf test
 !
 default-metric 1
 vrf test
  redistribute connected metric 1
  address-family ipv4 unicast
 !
 default-metric 1
!
end

Or route-policy incl 'set ospf-metric'

route-policy metric-test
  set ospf-metric 1
  pass
end-policy
!
router ospf 666
 cost 1
 redistribute connected route-policy metric-test
 address-family ipv4 unicast
 vrf test
 !
 default-metric 1
 vrf test
  redistribute connected route-policy metric-test
  address-family ipv4 unicast
 !
 default-metric 1
!
end
bestPracticeTitle: No Redistribution Metrics Defined for OSPF

bestPracticeCaveat: If this is a known, temporary situation then ignore for the device.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
If the BGP peer should be enabled then issue "no shutdown" for that neighbor. 
If the BGP peer is no longer needed (i.e. obsolete configuration) then remove the BGP neighbor.
bestPracticeDescription: Shutdown (BGP) is used to terminate any active session for the specified neighbor and remove all associated routing information. Use of the shutdown command with a neighbor group or session group may suddenly terminate a large number of BGP neighbor sessions because all neighbors using the neighbor group or session group may be affected. The shutdown should be a temporarily configuration change, which is expected to be within the configuration only a limited amount of time. This rule checks the presence of BGP neighbor shutdown.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Review the shutdown BGP neighbors within the configuration and determine if these BGP neighbors have become obsolete.  The BGP configuration for eBGP neighbors needs manual synchronization between the two peers, so it is a common practice to leave these neighbors under shutdown until the joint maintenance window where those are brought up.  Could see the same if physical RR is being migrated.

bestPracticeCaveat: Prevent of monitoring via SNMP system owned MIBs: eg CISCO-FABRIC-HFR-MIB Traps
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: snmp-server community  RO SystemOwner
bestPracticeDescription: System Resources like Fabric MIB have counter values that can be polled, but have a prerequisite to have implemented the use of a community sting with 'SystemOwner' privileges in order to be able read the MIB values. This has to be specifically configured. When the command snmp-server community is entered with the SystemOwner keyword, SNMP access is granted to all SDRs in the system. Only one community string can be configured with SystemOwner privileges. For example: snmp-server community mrtg RO SystemOwner Once this is in place, you are able to extract stats from the following CISCO-Fabric-HFR-MIB OIDs. The counters that you typically want to watch out for are the UCE counters (cfhPlaneStatsRxUCECells.1-8) as these values typically indicate a failed or failing link. Occasional CE or PE counter increments are also 'normal' but one should bear in mind that with a single system chassis, these counters should not be incrementing very much unless Fabric Module IOR-test is been performed. Fabric MIB examples are: - CISCO-Fabric-HFR-MIB - CISCO-Fabric-Mcast-MIB
bestPracticeRecommendation: Configure at least one (1) snmp-server community string configured with 'SystemOwner' privileges.
bestPracticeTitle: No snmp-server community string configured with SystemOwner privileges

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Enable BGP authentication for each eBGP peer.
bestPracticeDescription: For security reasons, it is advisable to utilize BGP authentication for BGP peers. You can prevent your router from receiving fraudulent route updates by configuring neighbor router authentication.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable BGP authentication for all eBGP peers.
bestPracticeTitle: BGP Authentication for eBGP Peers not Configured

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configuration as below:-

!
policy-map Test
 class Test
  priority level 1
  police rate percent 50
   exceed-action drop
!
bestPracticeDescription: Traffic policing manages the maximum rate of traffic through a token bucket algorithm. The token bucket algorithm uses user-configured values to determine the maximum rate of traffic allowed on an interface at a given moment in time. The token bucket algorithm is affected by all traffic entering or leaving the interface (depending on where the traffic policy with traffic policing is configured) and is useful in managing network bandwidth in cases where several large packets are sent in the same traffic stream.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to prevent any traffic class from starving any other traffic class. If a traffic class has a priority keyword, add a policer that drops exceeded traffic.
bestPracticeTitle: Traffic Class May Starve Other Traffic Class(es)

If interface preconfigurations are residing for a long period of time, this may indicate the hardware has been removed and has not been replaced or replaced by other type medium or port density.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Verify the interface preconfigurations are valid and related to upcoming network changes.

bestPracticeCaveat:
If no separate 1G interface is available, for example, all modules are 10GE linecards, then there are two other options that can be used instead. 
1.) The next option is to use the management interfaces. Do not connect the supervisor management interfaces back to back for the peer-keepalive. 
2.) The last option is to use an upstream L3 network for peer-keepalive. Do not carry the peer-keepalive communication over the peer-link.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configuration as given below :
!
feature vpc
interface port-channel12
  vpc peer-link
!
vpc domain 1
  peer-keepalive destination 172.23.145.217 source 172.23.145.218
!
interface mgmt1
  ip address 172.23.145.218/24
!
bestPracticeDescription: Associate a peer-keepalive link to a separate virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) instance that is mapped to a Layer 3 interface in each vPC peer device. If you do not configure a separate VRF, the system uses the management VRF by default. However, if you use the management interfaces for the peer-keepalive link, you must put a management switch connected to both the active and standby management ports on each vPC peer device
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to use separate VRF and front panel ports for peer-keepalive link (1G is more than adequate).
bestPracticeTitle: vPC peer keepalive not configured on separate physical interface

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Under 'router rip' or under 'vrf' configure 'nsf'. configuration... router rip nsf vrf xyz nsf ! !
bestPracticeDescription: To configure nonstop forwarding (NSF) on Routing Information Protocol (RIP) routes after a RIP process shutdown or restart, use the 'nsf' command in the appropriate configuration mode. When using the 'nsf' command, NSF lifetime is automatically set to two times the update time (with a minimum value of 60 seconds). The RIP process must reconverge within this time. If the convergence exceeds the NSF lifetime, routes are purged from the Routing Information Base (RIB) and NSF may fail. Default: NSF RIP is disabled.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Under 'router rip' or under 'vrf'-instances configure 'nsf'.
bestPracticeTitle: router rip nsf disabled

bestPracticeCaveat: Some applications, such as PPP, NTP require only two-way passwords because they must decrypt the stored password for their own function, such as sending the password in a packet.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
configure:
 user-name 
  secret [password]
  no password [password]
bestPracticeDescription: The username password is used to access privileged mode on a device. It should be protected with the username command secret. Using the username command secret implements a stronger encryption algorithm than the username command password, and should be used in lieu of the latter. In the event both commands are configured on a device under username, IOS XR will use the secret. Also a username is to be expected to be present, in case that Radius or Tacacs+ is not reachable, then the authentication should fall back to the local defined username(s). This is been accomplished through the local parameter within the aaa authorization command.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Use username "secret" only to protect the privileged mode password.  Username password is has a weaker encryption schema in respect to secret and is therefore not the best adequate protection methodology.
bestPracticeTitle: A user account is not protected with MD5

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: logging {ip-address | hostname}
bestPracticeDescription: Logging system messages to a syslog server is an effective form of fault management. Reviewing the logs and checking for critical messages are important aspects for troubleshooting and incident investigation. To specify a system logging (syslog) server host as the recipient of syslog messages, use the logging command in global configuration mode. Use the logging command to identify a syslog server host to receive messages. By issuing this command more than once, you build a list of syslog servers that receive messages. When syslog messages are sent to a syslog server, the Cisco IOS XR software includes a numerical message identifier in syslog messages. The message identifier is cumulative and sequential. The numerical identifier included in syslog messages sent to syslog servers provides a means to determine if any messages have been lost.
bestPracticeRecommendation: To support redundancy, there should be at least two syslog servers in the network.  Set up a backup syslog server, preferably at a different location from the primary syslog server. Add this backup syslog server as a destination to the device.
bestPracticeTitle: No redundant syslog server

bestPracticeCaveat: NTP is not supported on the 4-MB 2900-XL switches. In addition, this rule only checks that the device is configured correctly for NTP, but there is no guarantee that the devices actually have synchronized clocks. For example, if ntp broadcast client is used, it relies on another device on the segment to be broadcast time into the network.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configuration as below 

ntp source loopback1
ntp server 10.255.255.3
bestPracticeDescription: NTP is designed to synchronize the time on a network of machines. NTP runs over the User Datagram Protocol (UDP), using port 123 as both the source and destination, which in turn runs over IP. NTP Version 3 is used to synchronize timekeeping among a set of distributed time servers and clients. A set of nodes on a network are identified and configured with NTP and the nodes form a synchronization subnet, sometimes referred to as an overlay network. While multiple masters (primary servers) may exist, there is no requirement for an election protocol. An NTP network usually gets its time from an authoritative time source, such as a radio clock or an atomic clock attached to a time server. NTP then distributes this time across the network. An NTP client makes a transaction with its server over its polling interval (from 64 to 1024 seconds) which dynamically changes over time depending on the network conditions between the NTP server and the client. The other situation occurs when the router communicates to a bad NTP server (for example, NTP server with large dispersion); the router also increases the poll interval. No more than one NTP transaction per minute is needed to synchronize two machines. It is not possible to adjust the NTP poll interval on a router. NTP uses the concept of a stratum to describe how many NTP hops away a machine is from an authoritative time source. For example, a stratum 1 time server has a radio or atomic clock directly attached to it. It then sends its time to a stratum 2 time server through NTP, and so on. A machine running NTP automatically chooses the machine with the lowest stratum number that it is configured to communicate with using NTP as its time source. This strategy effectively builds a self-organizing tree of NTP speakers. NTP performs well over the non-deterministic path lengths of packet-switched networks, because it makes robust estimates of the following three key variables in the relationship between a client and a time server.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Enable NTP on all devices in the network.
bestPracticeTitle: NTP not enabled

bestPracticeCaveat: This is not necessary if OSPF is not configured on the loopback interface.

Configuration as given below :
!
interface Loopback0
!
interface Loopback0

!

bestPracticeRecommendation: The recommendation is to always configure "ip ospf network point-to-point" on any loopback interface. This ensures that the correct mask shows up in neighboring routing table entries.
bestPracticeTitle: OSPF Point-to-Point Not Configured on Loopback Interfaces.

Configure:
ntp
server ipv4-address_A

bestPracticeDescription: This rule checks if there are at least more than 2 NTP servers configured. NTP server redundancy ensures that if a device cannot access a NTP server for time synchronization, another is available to maintain the clock. The Best Practice is to implement NTP-servers at different geographical locations. No servers are configured by default. If a server is configured, the default NTP version number is 4, no authentication key is used, and the source IP address is taken from the outgoing interface. minpoll interval: 6 seconds maxpoll interval: 10 seconds The value for the minpoll keyword must be less than or equal to the value for the maxpoll keyword.
