Router(config)# et-analytics
Router(config-et-analytics)# ip flow-export destination 10.1.1.1 2055
Router(config-et-analytics)# inactive time 10
bestPracticeDescription: Encrypted Traffic Analytics (ETA) uses machine learning on an application to determine the flow characteristics such as malware analysis and crypto audit.ETA supports multiple templates for the configuration export. There is one template per ETA attribute and ETA sends individual attribute detail in each template during the export. Sequence of Packet Length and Times (SPLT) and Initial Data Packet (IDP) are stored in separate templates, which are used to generate NetFlow records.Both these NetFlow records are sent for a given application flow.These templates are sent whenever the data is ready.This helps NetFlow collector to interpret data with correct attribute values.The exporter destination and port is going to be common for all interfaces and this value is provided in the global et-analytics configuration command.The scale number for ETA is 2000 flows per second.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to configure Encrypted Traffic Analytics (ETA) to detect malicious traffic on devices.

bestPracticeDescription: Each key definition within the keychain must specify a time interval for which that key is activated; for example, lifetime. Then, during a given key's lifetime, routing update packets are sent with this activated key. Keys cannot be used during time periods for which they are not activated. The lifetime of a key is defined by the following options: Start-time?Specifies the absolute time. End-time?Specifies the absolute time that is relative to the start-time or infinite time.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco Recommends to use lifetime of a key each time any key management implemented with any protocol.Its must include valid time frame with start time and end time.

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Do not raise an Exception for below snippet :-
!
multicast-routing
 address-family ipv4|ipv6 
  no accounting per-prefix
!

Raise an Exception for below snippet :-

multicast-routing
 address-family ipv4|ipv6 
  accounting per-prefix
!
bestPracticeDescription: Cisco IOSXR Software counters are always present. To enable per-prefix counters only in hardware, use the accounting per-prefix command. When per-prefix counters are enabled, existing, and new (S, G) and (*, G) routes are assigned ingress counters, except for the following: Default multicast routes IPv4 (*, G) routes configured with prefix length less than 32. IPv6 (*, G) routes configured with prefix length less than 128. For troubleshooting purposes, you can configure accounting-per-prefix under multicast-routing mode to enable accounting for multicast routing for a limited number of routes temporarily.
bestPracticeRecommendation: You must disable accounting per-prefix immediately after troubleshooting.
bestPracticeTitle: Checks to disable the accounting per prefix for the multicast routes

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
The following example shows a basic remote triggered IPv4 black hole filtering configuration on an edge router:

community-set ddos-comm
 1:666
end-set
!
route-policy RTBH-drop
 if community matches-any ddos-comm then
  set next-hop 192.0.2.1
  set local-preference 200
 endif
end-policy
!
router static
 address-family ipv4 unicast
 192.0.2.1/32 Null0
!
router bgp 65000
 neighbor 191.0.0.2
 remote-as 65188
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-policy RTBH-drop in
!

Note: In Cisco IOS XR Software, the Null 0 interface always exists and it never sends ICMP unreachable messages.
bestPracticeDescription: When the origin of an attack (for example, by an analysis of NetFlow data), containment mechanisms, such as Access Control Lists (ACLs) can be configured. When attack traffic is detected and classified, appropriate ACLs can be created and deployed to the necessary routers. Because this manual process can be time-consuming and complex, in many instances, Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) is used in order to propagate drop information to all routers quickly and efficiently. RTBH filtering is a technique that sets the next hop of the victim's IP address to the null interface. Traffic destined to the victim is dropped on ingress into the network. Remote Triggered Black Hole Filtering uses the CEF process (fast path) to drop undesirable IPv4 traffic before it is forwarded to the network.
bestPracticeRecommendation: As a part of securing the data plane to mitigate the distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks within a single interior gateway protocol (IGP) domain, it is recommended to configure RTBH filtering.
bestPracticeTitle: Remote Triggered Black Hole Filtering not configured for IPv4 Traffic

bestPracticeDescription: The SSH port forwarding feature is disabled, by default. We can enable the feature by using the ssh server port-forwarding local command in the XR Config mode.With this feature enabled, the SSH client on a local host forwards the traffic coming on a given port to the specified host and port on a remote server, through an encrypted SSH channel. Legacy applications that do not otherwise support data encryption can leverage this functionality to ensure network security and confidentiality to the traffic that is sent to remote application servers.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to enable this feature as SSH port forwarding is a method of forwarding the otherwise insecure TCP/IP connections from the SSH client to server through a secure SSH channel.

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configure a non-default SRGB range.
The default SRGB in IOS XR has a size of 8000 starting from label value 16000. The default range is 16000 to 23,999. 

!
segment-routing global-block 16000 23999
!
bestPracticeDescription: The Segment Routing Global Block (SRGB) is a range of labels reserved for Segment Routing global segments. A prefix-SID is advertised as a domain-wide unique index. The prefix-SID index points to a unique label within the SRGB range. The index is zero-based, meaning that the first index is 0.When you define a non-default SRGB range, there might be a label conflict (for example, if labels are already allocated, statically or dynamically, in the new SRGB range). The following system log message indicates a label conflict: %ROUTING-ISIS-4-SRGB_ALLOC_FAIL : SRGB allocation failed: 'SRGB reservation not successful for [16000,80000], SRGB (16000 80000, SRGB_ALLOC_CONFIG_PENDING, 0x2) (So far 16 attempts). Make sure label range is free' To remove this conflict, you must reload the router to release the currently allocated labels and to allocate the new SRGB.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to use the default SRGB range to avoid a potential router reload due to label conflicts, and assuming that the default SRGB size is large enough
bestPracticeTitle: Default SRGB range Not used in segment routing global block

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Example configure a non-default SRGB range.
The default SRLB has a size of 1000 starting from label value 15000; therefore, the default SRLB range goes from 15000 to 15,999.

!
segment-routing local-block 15000 15999
!
bestPracticeDescription: When we define a non-default SRLB range, there might be a label conflict (for example, if labels are already allocated, statically or dynamically, in the new SRLB range). In this case, the new SRLB range will be accepted, but not applied (pending state). The previous SRLB range (active) will continue to be in use.To remove this conflict, We must reload the router to release the currently allocated labels and to allocate the new SRLB.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends to use the default SRGB range to avoid a potential router reload due to label conflicts, and assuming that the default SRGB size is large enough
bestPracticeTitle: Default SRLB range Not used in segment routing local block

Although it is recommended to use the NSF lifetime default values. Generally, configure the IGMP NSF and PIM NSF lifetime values to equal or exceed the query or join query interval. For example, if you set the IGMP query interval to 120 seconds, set the IGMP NSF lifetime to 120 seconds (or greater). bestPracticeCaveat:
Use of the no vstack global configuration command to disable the Smart Install client feature was introduced with the fix for Cisco defect CSCtj75729 (Ability to shut Smart Install default service on TCP port 4786). If a Cisco IOS or IOS XE Software release supports the Smart Install client feature but the no vstack command does not exist, the release does not contain the fix for Cisco defect CSCtj75729.

The 'no vstack' command does not persist across reload due to Cisco defect CSCvd99197 in the following releases of Cisco IOS and IOS XE:

- Cisco Catalyst 4500 and 4500-X Series Switches: 3.9.2E/15.2(5)E2
- Cisco Catalyst 6500 Series Switches (supports the Smart Install Director feature only): 15.1(2)SY11, 15.2(1)SY5, 15.2(2)SY3
- Cisco Industrial Ethernet 4000 Series Switches: 15.2(5)E2, 15.2(5)E2a

If you are running any of these releases, Cisco recommends that you upgrade or downgrade to a non-affected release or put automation in place to reconfigure the no vstack command after every reload of the device.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Configure 'no vstack'
bestPracticeDescription: Several researchers have reported on the use of Smart Install (SMI) protocol messages toward Smart Install clients, also known as integrated branch clients (IBC), allowing an unauthenticated, remote attacker to change the startup-config file and force a reload of the device, load a new IOS image on the device, and execute high-privilege CLI commands on switches running Cisco IOS and IOS XE Software.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Customers who do not use the Cisco Smart Install feature, and who are running a release of Cisco IOS or Cisco IOS XE Software where the command is available, should disable the Smart Install feature (either Client or Director) with the configuration command 'no vstack'.
bestPracticeTitle: Smart Install Enabled

Rather than have the router open and close a TCP connection to the server each time it must communicate, the single-connection option maintains a single open connection between the router and the server. The single connection is more efficient because it allows the server to handle a higher number of TACACS operations
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends that rather than have the router open and close a TCP connection to the server each time it must communicate, the single-connection option maintains a single open connection between the router and the server. The single connection is more efficient because it allows the server to handle a higher number of TACACS operations.

bestPracticeCaveat: Some countries have cryptography restrictions and may dictate which encryption algorithms can be used.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure "encryption aes" or better under the crypto isakmp policy and aes-128 or higher under the transform-set.
!
crypto isakmp policy 2
 encryption aes *
! 
crypto ipsec transform-set (name) esp-aes *
!
bestPracticeDescription: Symmetric key algorithms use the same key for encryption and decryption. Examples include 3DES and AES. 3DES, which consists of three sequential Data Encryption Standard (DES) encryption-decryptions, is a legacy algorithm. This designation means that 3DES provides a marginal but acceptable security level, but its keys should be renewed relatively often. Because of its small key size, DES is no longer secure and should be avoided. RC4 should be avoided too. AES with 128-bit keys provides adequate protection for sensitive information. AES with 256-bit keys is required to protect classified information of higher importance. esp-aes 192 - ESP with the 192-bit AES encryption algorithm. esp-aes 256 - ESP with the 256-bit AES encryption algorithm. esp-des - ESP with the 56-bit Data Encryption Standard (DES) encryption algorithm. esp-3des - ESP with the 168-bit DES encryption algorithm (3DES or Triple DES).
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommends that at a minimum, AES-128 should be utilized for encryption.  However, consider using AES-192 or AES-256 for maximum security.
bestPracticeTitle: DES or 3DES Encryption Configured in VPN Policy

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: Configure "hash sha" or better under the crypto isakmp policy and utilize esp-sha-hmac under the transform set.
bestPracticeDescription: Hash algorithms are also called digital fingerprinting algorithms. They are irreversible functions that provide a fixed-size hash based on various inputs. Irreversibility and collision resistance are necessary attributes for successful hash functions. Examples of hash functions are Secure Hash Algorithm 1 (SHA-1) and SHA-256.Message Digest 5 (MD5) is a hash function that is insecure and should be avoided. SHA-1 is a legacy algorithm and thus is adequately secure. SHA-256 provides adequate protection for sensitive information. On the other hand, SHA-384 is required to protect classified information of higher importance. Hashed Message Authentication Code (HMAC) is a construction that uses a secret key and a hash function to provide a message authentication code (MAC) for a message. HMAC is used for integrity verification. HMAC-MD5, which uses MD5 as its hash function, is a legacy algorithm. Note that MD5 as a hash function itself is not secure. It provides adequate security today but its keys should be renewed relatively often. Alternatively, the NIST-recommended HMAC function is HMAC-SHA-1.
bestPracticeRecommendation: At a minimum, SHA-1 should be used for hashing.  However, consider using IKEv2's next gen hashing algorithms such as SHA-256, SHA-348 and SHA-512.
bestPracticeTitle: MD5 Hashing Configured in VPN Policy

bestPracticeDescription: The basic input and output system (BIOS) tests and initializes the hardware components of a system and boots the operating system from a storage device. A typical computational system has several BIOS settings that control the system?s behaviour. Some of these settings are directly related to the performance of the system. Workload Configuration can be configured to the following options: Balanced I/O Sensitive Non-uniform memory access (NUMA), (can be set only via GUI) Uniform memory access (UMA), (can be set only via GUI) Platform Default ? The BIOS uses the value for this attribute contained in the BIOS defaults for the server type and vendor. By default, I/O Sensitive is enabled.

bestPracticeTitle: Workload Configuration not set to Balanced in Processor Bios setting

bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
router isis 
 set-overload-bit on-startup wait-for-bgp
!
bestPracticeDescription: In ISP environment where routers running BGP with IS-IS as an IGP, whenever there is a maintenance planned for the device upgrade or migration the device may have to be reloaded. When the device reloads the IS-IS and BGP have to converge for the router to resume back to it's normal operations. Generally BGP takes a longer time than IS-IS to converge. The target device which was reloaded must not be used as transient router until BGP has converged, because it might create a transient black hole until BGP converges. To handle this scenario we can configure "set-overload-bit on-startup wait-for-bgp" before the device undergoes reload. After the load with link state advertisements are sent to the neighbor routers the overload bit is set and this indicates to the neighbor routers that this device is not ready for the transient traffic yet. The overload bit is reset once all the BGP sessions are converged and router can return back to its normal operation.
bestPracticeRecommendation: In ISP environment where routers running BGP with IS-IS as an IGP, whenever there is a maintenance planned for the device upgrade or migration the device may have to be reloaded. When the device reloads the IS-IS and BGP have to converge for the router to resume back to it's normal operations. Generally BGP takes a longer time than IS-IS to converge. The target device which was reloaded must not be used as transient router until BGP has converged, because it might create a transient black hole until BGP converges.
bestPracticeTitle: Avoid transient black holing - ISPs running BGP and IS-IS

bestPracticeCaveat:
http://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/routers/asr-9000-series-aggregation-services-routers/116727-troubleshoot-punt-00.html

Please note :This is command valid for 32-Bit only
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Configure "fabric fia soft-error-monitor  location " in IOS-XR configuration on ASR 9000

Example configuration :-

RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR(admin-config)#fabric fia soft-error-monitor 1 location ?        
  WORD  Fully qualified line card specification
bestPracticeDescription: When the FPGA memory is corrupted by a soft error it will cause inconsistnet behavior or deviation from the intended functionality. In these cases the FPGA will remain in the failed state unless a reload of the configuration memory (image) is done. Soft errors are nonpermanent errors that cause the state machine to be out of sync. These are seen as Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC), Frame Check Sequence (FCS), or errored packets on the fabric side of the NP or on the ingress side of the FIA.An option needs to be implemented to recover from such scenarios, which can occur in the fleld over the operational life of the card, automatically. In general, in order to recover from these errors, a Linecard reload is required. A bug was opened for this issue - Cisco bug ID CSCui15435. The fix to this Defect allows a user to configure the desired action following such errors: (admin-config) fabric fia soft-error-monitor location Level 1: shut ports Level 2: reload LC The recovery action is always a LC reload. The difference between option 1 and 2 is whether the customer prefers: 1. Automated shutdown of interfaces and manually reload the linecard at a later stage. 2. Immediate automated LC reload Without any configuration, no action is taken by default. Hence, it is recommended to run the command and choose an option for all LCs, so that there will not be any blackholes when the FPGA memory is corrupted in any of ASR 9K LC's
bestPracticeRecommendation: Cisco recommended to have Recovery mechanism configuration enabled for FPGA soft errors on ASR 9000 Linecards. Hence, configure "fabric fia soft-error-monitor  location " on all linecards in ASR 9000
bestPracticeTitle: Enabling recovery mechanism configuration for FPGA soft errors on ASR 9000 Linecards

bestPracticeCaveat: CSCwd35358
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction: In case the best practice limit is exceeded the corrective action should be to merge some of the existing OSPF processes, this can be done by moving all VRFs to a single OSPF process.
bestPracticeDescription: The maximum amount of OSPF processes that can be configured in an ASR9k router is 19 processes, the IOS XR OS running in the ASR9k will show the following error messages when trying to configure more than the 10 OSPF process limit: RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR-9010-A(config-ospf)#do sh protocols | i OSPF | utility wc Fri Dec 2 08:07:32.782 UTC 19 76 485 RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR-9010-A(config-ospf)#exit RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR-9010-A(config-ospf)#router ospf 11 RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR-9010-A(config-ospf)#commit Fri Dec 2 08:07:52.339 UTC RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:Dec 2 08:07:52.397 UTC: placed[398]: %OS-PLACED-3-PROCESS_EXHAUSTED : Exhausted all instances of placeable process ospf possible % Failed to commit one or more configuration items during a pseudo-atomic operation. All changes made have been reverted. Please issue 'show configuration failed [inheritance]' from this session to view the errors RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR-9010-A(config-ospf)#show configuration failed Fri Dec 2 08:08:08.765 UTC !! SEMANTIC ERRORS: This configuration was rejected by !! the system due to semantic errors. The individual !! errors with each failed configuration command can be !! found below. router ospf 11 !!% 'CfgMgr' detected the 'fatal' condition 'This configuration has not been verified and can not be accepted by the system.' ! end In IOS the limit of OSPF processes that can run simultaneously is 32, this is due to the fact that some PE-CE connectivity requires an OSPF process per VRF, in IOS-XR the recommendation is to integrate all the PE-CE VRFs into a single OSPF process, the actual maximum limit of OSPF processes is 19 but the actual recommendation is to configure a maximum of 4 OSPF processes as a best practice for optimal performance. This is something to consider, specially if you're migrating from an IOS platform like the Cisco 7600 router to an ASR9k router. When reviewing a router you can issue the following commands to validate the amount of OSPF processes configured: RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR-9010-A#show run | i router ospf Fri Dec 2 08:15:25.093 UTC Building configuration... router ospf 1 router ospf 2 router ospf 3 router ospf 4 router ospf 5 router ospf 6 router ospf 7 router ospf 8 router ospf 9 router ospf 10 router ospf 84 router ospf 99 router ospf 100 router ospf 111 router ospf 1000 router ospf 1664 router ospf 21928 router ospf shache router ospf ROEMMANU RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:ASR-9010-A#
bestPracticeRecommendation: The recommended Best Practice is not to exceed 4 OSPF processes running on the router simultaneously.
bestPracticeTitle: ASR9k OSPF Process Limit

bestPracticeCaveat: In most scenarios it is considered a good idea to use this feature, except when TCP connections between the router and the server are very unreliable.
bestPracticeCorrectiveAction:
Make sure that the tacacs-server config has this line:
tacacs-server host x.x.x.x  <<<
 single-connection
!
bestPracticeDescription: The single-connection keyword specifies a single connection. Rather than have the router open and close a TCP connection to the server each time it must communicate, the single-connection option maintains a single open connection between the router and the server. The single connection is more efficient because it allows the server to handle a higher number of TACACS operations.
bestPracticeRecommendation: Rather than have the router open and close a TCP connection to the server each time it must communicate, the single-connection option maintains a single open connection between the router and the server. The single connection is more efficient because it allows the server to handle a higher number of TACACS operations.
bestPracticeTitle: Tacacs Single Connection for IOS-XR
